content.claim: Hungary exhibits negative demographic momentum with youth cohorts contracted to 14.4% of the population, presaging structural fiscal and staffing pressures that constrain school-network decisions across regions independent of whether individual schools remain open.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: 'Negative demographic momentum' is inferred from cohort structure; does not directly measure fiscal impact magnitude per region or school type
The relationship between contracted youth cohorts and regional teacher surplus/redistribution is stated as a possibility, not a measured outcome
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
